title: tournefortia bojeri
intro: Tournefortia bojeri, also known as the Mahoe or Hawaiian Tournefortia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, providing benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Tournefortia bojeri is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for various health purposes.
